title: Contribution #2 — docstring/API docs for a permissive genomics tool
context: With the pilot (pilot-007) proving the end-to-end pipeline, this contribution extends the pattern to a second permissive genomics tool that has passed the gate (triage-009). Docstring and API documentation gaps are a primary barrier for newcomers to cancer genomics tools: missing parameter descriptions, no return-type annotations, and undocumented edge cases lead to incorrect usage and irreproducible analyses. The target tool must already have a PASS gate artifact from triage-009. riskTier is low: developer-facing docs on a permissive tool using shipped/synthetic data with no biological claims requiring domain review.
objective: Author and contribute merged docstring/API documentation for a permissive genomics tool that has passed the gate, following the templates and canonical contribution model, with coverage delta recorded.
acceptanceCriteria: Target tool has a PASS gate artifact from triage-009 confirming OSI-permissive license, acceptable contribution policy (DCO preferred), passing for-profit-vehicle check, and cancer-data guardrail clearance.
Issue-first alignment confirmed with the maintainer before authoring.
Docstring/API docs authored following the docstring template (template-003), covering all public API surfaces in scope.
Coverage delta (doc-completeness before and after, using interrogate/pydocstyle for Python or roxygen/tools::undoc for R) recorded in the gate/provenance artifact.
License + Data-provenance reviewer sign-off and Technical reviewer sign-off obtained.
Contribution merged upstream; outcome artifact committed at outcomes/onco-research-software-docs-contrib-011.json with merge-commit URL and coverage before/after.
output: Docstring/API documentation contribution merged upstream for a gate-passed permissive genomics tool, with coverage delta and outcome artifact recorded.
